title: ageratina choricephaloides
intro: Ageratina choricephaloides, also known as Mexican mint or Hierba de los Muertos, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Ageratina choricephaloides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried leaves can also be used in herbal remedies and supplements.
